name: Wright-Patterson AFB
description: Home of Air Materiel Command; headquarters of Project Sign (1947), Project Grudge (1949), and Project Blue Book (1952-1969). Nearly all Blue Book case files were processed and stored here.

name: Pentagon
description: DoD headquarters. Site of AATIP operations (2007-2012) and subsequent UAPTF and AARO offices. Luis Elizondo directed AATIP from here before resigning in 2017.

name: Kirtland AFB
description: Key AFOSI counterintelligence hub. Site of documented 1980 UAP incidents investigated by Richard Doty. Sandia National Laboratories co-located; adjacent to Manzano weapons storage area linked to multiple AFOSI reports.

name: Area 51 / Groom Lake
description: Classified Nevada Test and Training Range facility. Alleged site of multiple USAP programs. Linked to UAP retrieval claims by multiple whistleblowers including Bob Lazar, who described S-4 operations south of the main facility.

name: Skinwalker Ranch
description: Utah property purchased by Robert Bigelow in 1996. Operated as the primary NIDS field research site and subsequently the anchor location for the AAWSAP contract. Multi-year on-site investigation by Bigelow Aerospace and DIA-contracted personnel.

name: Dugway Proving Ground
description: DoD test range in the Utah desert. Associated with classified aerospace programs and multiple UAP sighting reports from military and civilian sources in the surrounding region.

name: Holloman AFB
description: New Mexico air base adjacent to White Sands Missile Range. Appeared in multiple Project Blue Book reports. Associated with alleged 1954 contact claims and several AFOSI investigations.
